scenario: Nimbus Bank normalises customer ids for 19000 transactions per day in Camunda 8.8. A FEEL expression must uppercase the variable customerId and then return only the first 8 characters of the result for use as a routing key in a downstream business rule task on the parent scope.
question: Which FEEL string functions produce this uppercased 8-character routing key?

options.text: Use substring(upper case(customerId), 1, 8) which uppercases the string first and then takes the first 8 characters using a 1-indexed start position
Use customerId.toUpperCase().substring(0, 8) which chains FEEL string methods and uses a 0-indexed start position similar to most other languages
Use upper case(substring(customerId, 0, 8)) which takes 8 characters from a 0-indexed start position and then uppercases the resulting string
Use upper(left(customerId, 8)) which calls FEEL's left(text, length) helper and then applies the upper(text) function to the resulting string

a.text: Correct. FEEL string functions are upper case(string) and substring(string, start, length) with 1-indexed start positions.

b.text: Incorrect. FEEL is not method-chained; functions take the value as a positional argument and start positions are 1-indexed.

c.text: Incorrect. FEEL substring is 1-indexed, not 0-indexed; the start position 0 is out of range.

d.text: Incorrect. FEEL does not provide upper or left helpers; the documented names are upper case and substring.

explanation: FEEL string functions: upper case(string) and substring(string, start, length) with 1-indexed start positions.
